page: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-6 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine instance configured with an x86_64 CPU architecture. The server features 6 dedicated vCPUs and 22.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 3.67 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it includes a fractional GPU allocation consisting of 1/8 of an NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPU. This instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The fractional GPU allocation provides a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that require GPU acceleration but do not demand a full, dedicated GPU frame. By partitioning the NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000, the instance delivers hardware-accelerated rendering and compute capabilities at a lower resource density and cost profile compared to full-GPU instances. However, the lack of local storage means all data persistence must rely on network-attached storage, which introduces external I/O considerations.

This server configuration is suited for entry-level graphics processing, remote workstation hosting, lightweight 3D rendering, and basic GPU-accelerated compute tasks. It is also appropriate for development and testing environments where applications are optimized for NVIDIA RTX architectures before scaling to larger, non-fractional GPU instances.
description: Google Cloud Platform g4-standard-6 is a graphics-optimized virtual machine featuring 6 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture and 22.0 GB of system memory. It is equipped with a fractional GPU allocation of 1/8 of an NVIDIA RTX Pro 6000 GPU, offering a cost-effective entry point for hardware-accelerated tasks without the expense of a full GPU. The instance does not feature local storage, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence, and includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile is designed for entry-level graphics rendering, remote desktop hosting, and lightweight GPU-accelerated development workloads.
